learnings.text: The P24-P39 principle files also included wisdom_roots_operational_view.md which is valuable N5OS content, so I kept it in the payload (14 principle files instead of 13)
install.py successfully handles merging mode and conflict detection - 37 conflicts shown properly in dry-run, 39 files installed with --force
The verify.py script correctly detects placeholder markers ({{, }}, WORKSPACE, HANDLE) that need personalization - this is expected behavior until D3 implements the personalization engine
Jinja2 templates work well for N5OS personalization - allows flexible instance configuration while maintaining structured output

Template engine without Jinja2 dependency can handle basic conditional blocks, for loops, and filters. Key was processing loops before variable substitution and handling {{ var | filter }} patterns within loop content.
